title: Azure Stack HCI Cluster Validation and Quorum Health
app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services, Windows Event Forwarding
spl:
index=azure_stack_hci sourcetype="azurestackhci:cluster" earliest=-24h
| stats latest(validation_status) as val, latest(witness_reachable) as wit by cluster_name
| where val!="Passed" OR wit="false" OR wit=0
| table cluster_name, val, wit
description: Failed cluster validation or quorum issues can pause live migration and stretch failover. Centralizing validation results and witness reachability in Splunk reduces time to restore majority before a second fault impacts VMs.
value: Failed cluster validation or quorum issues can pause live migration and stretch failover. Centralizing validation results and witness reachability in Splunk reduces time to restore majority before a second fault impacts VMs.
implementation: (1) Ship Cluster-Witness and validation cmdlet output from automation to HEC or scripted input; (2) alert on any non-passed validation or witness unreachable; (3) document witness repair steps for file share vs cloud witness.
visualization: Status grid (cluster validation), Table (failing checks), Single value (clusters at risk).

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services, Windows Event Forwarding.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=azure_stack_hci` `sourcetype="azurestackhci:cluster"` with fields `cluster_name`, `validation_status`, `quorum_type`, `witness_reachable`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ship Cluster-Witness and validation cmdlet output from automation to HEC or scripted input; (2) alert on any non-passed validation or witness unreachable; (3) document witness repair steps for file share vs cloud witness.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=azure_stack_hci sourcetype="azurestackhci:cluster" earliest=-24h
| stats latest(validation_status) as val, latest(witness_reachable) as wit by cluster_name
| where val!="Passed" OR wit="false" OR wit=0
| table cluster_name, val, wit
```

Understanding this SPL

**Azure Stack HCI Cluster Validation and Quorum Health** — Failed cluster validation or quorum issues can pause live migration and stretch failover. Centralizing validation results and witness reachability in Splunk reduces time to restore majority before a second fault impacts VMs.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=azure_stack_hci` `sourcetype="azurestackhci:cluster"` with fields `cluster_name`, `validation_status`, `quorum_type`, `witness_reachable`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services, Windows Event Forwarding. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: azure_stack_hci; **sourcetype**: azurestackhci:cluster. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=azure_stack_hci, sourcetype="azurestackhci:cluster", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by cluster_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where val!="Passed" OR wit="false" OR wit=0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Azure Stack HCI Cluster Validation and Quorum Health**): table cluster_name, val, wit


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the same scope in the vendor HCI or compute console (UCS, vCenter, Nutanix Prism, and so on).

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Status grid (cluster validation), Table (failing checks), Single value (clusters at risk).

Scripted input (generic example)
This use case relies on a scripted input. In the app's local/inputs.conf add a stanza such as:

```ini
[script://$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/YourApp/bin/collect.sh]
interval = 300
sourcetype = your_sourcetype
index = main
disabled = 0
```

The script should print one event per line (e.g. key=value). Example minimal script (bash):

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Output metrics or events, one per line
echo "metric=value timestamp=$(date +%s)"
```

For full details (paths, scheduling, permissions), see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md
grandmaExplanation: Failed cluster validation or quorum issues can pause live migration and stretch failover — so hardware faults and capacity problems do not take services down.
hardware: Azure Stack HCI validated server catalog nodes
knownFalsePositives: Cluster events during planned hardware refresh, capacity adds, or scheduled maintenance modes.
